title: Israel Strikes Kill Iran's Intelligence Chief and Senior Security Official
subtitle: Overnight Israeli operations have targeted senior Iranian figures. Tehran has yet to respond formally but has vowed retaliation as strikes spread across the wider Middle East.

updates.label: Breaking
updates.headline: Israel Says Iran's Intelligence Minister Killed in Overnight Strike
updates.text: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz has confirmed that Esmail Khatib, Iran's Intelligence Minister, was eliminated in an overnight Israeli strike. Katz stated that Khatib's killing followed the death of top security official Ali Larijani the previous day. Iran has not yet officially commented on the claim, though security sources in the region have not disputed the reports.

updates.label: Update
updates.headline: Tehran Vows Retaliation Following Larijani's Death
updates.text: Senior Iranian officials have issued statements threatening a severe response following the killing of Ali Larijani, the country's top security chief, in yesterday's Israeli operation. While Tehran has not yet formally acknowledged the death of the intelligence minister, the tone from Iranian leadership has hardened considerably overnight, with officials warning that any further Israeli escalation will be met with a response that crosses new thresholds.

updates.label: Update
updates.headline: Israeli Strikes Hit Central Beirut as Lebanon Offensive Continues
updates.text: Israel has launched a series of airstrikes on Beirut, with at least one explosion flattening a multi-storey building in a central commercial district surrounded by hotels and businesses — well outside the southern suburbs traditionally associated with Hezbollah's presence. The strikes mark a significant geographic expansion of Israel's Lebanese offensive and have drawn immediate international condemnation.

updates.label: Update
updates.headline: Retaliatory Strikes Spread Across the Middle East
updates.text: Drone interceptions and explosions have been reported across multiple countries overnight including Dubai, Qatar, Kuwait, Iraq, Bahrain, and Saudi Arabia. In Tel Aviv, two people were killed in what Israeli authorities described as a retaliatory attack by Iran-aligned armed groups. The scale and geographic spread of the incidents suggests a significant and coordinated escalation by the axis of resistance.

updates.label: Context
updates.headline: US Military Used Bunker-Busting Bombs on Iranian Missile Sites
updates.text: The United States military has confirmed it deployed heavy bunker-busting bombs — including deep penetrator munitions — against Iranian missile launch infrastructure along the Strait of Hormuz. The vital oil shipping route has been effectively blocked by Iran since the conflict escalated, threatening global energy supply chains. The US strikes represent a significant direct military intervention by Washington in what had previously been framed as Israeli operations.

updates.label: Context
updates.headline: Who Was Esmail Khatib — Iran's Intelligence Chief
updates.text: Esmail Khatib served as Iran's Minister of Intelligence since 2021, overseeing the country's vast domestic and foreign intelligence apparatus. He was a senior figure within the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps network and was considered one of the architects of Iran's regional intelligence operations. His elimination, if confirmed, would represent one of the most significant blows to Iran's intelligence leadership in decades and follows the killing of Larijani — Iran's top national security official — in what analysts are describing as a systematic Israeli campaign to decapitate Tehran's security establishment.

updates.label: Quote
updates.headline: 'Leaving Home Is Like Gambling With My Life' — Tehran Resident
updates.text: Residents of Tehran describe living under constant anxiety as the situation deteriorates. One woman told Alamin Network's correspondent that daily life has become almost unbearable, saying that every time she steps outside she feels as though she is taking a gamble with her own survival. Shops in several districts of the capital have closed early and queues have formed at petrol stations as residents prepare for the possibility of prolonged disruption.

updates.label: Started
updates.headline: Live Blog Opens — Rapidly Developing Situation Across the Middle East
updates.text: Alamin Network is covering a rapidly evolving situation following overnight Israeli strikes that have claimed the lives of senior Iranian officials. Our international desk is tracking developments across Israel, Iran, Lebanon, and the wider region. Refresh for the latest updates.
